verdict_reason: The single disagreement (kṛpā) is well-explained: it identifies the specific Sanskrit term, names the three positions (Prabhupada's compassion, Mukundananda's pity, Prabhupada Purport's Gaudiya pejorative reading of kṛpā as 'material compassion / sign of ignorance of the real self'), and explicitly states what is doctrinally at stake ('the chapter's diagnosis hinges on whether Arjuna's pity is misdirected or merely overwhelming'). It names the axis (sectarian commentarial choice / metaphysical commitment to bodily-vs-soul reference of the term), is doctrinally substantive (explains why the difference matters), is one tight line, and does not pick a winner. Gold-standard quality.
